Note: This is fictional This is an odd lineup, starting on the 3rd October on Boomerang UK.

New Episodes of Be Cool Scooby Doo will run at 17:00 and 07:00. The morning slot will be an hour block
Talking Tom & Friends makes a weekday run at 16:00
Masha & the Bear will also runs on weekdays at 15:00
Oddbods finally gets shown on Weekdays, instead of being run only on weekends or occasions.
Gone from the schedule are Pink Panther & Pals, Inspector Gadget, LazyTown and Sonic Boom
Daytime lineup from 9:00 to 15:00 now consists of hour blocks
The Happos Family has been announced for this month, but no date confirmed.
UPDATE: NEW Show Grizzy and the Lemmings from October 10th at 16:30.
Chaotic joins the night schedule, replacing LazyTown at 4:30
The Cramp Twins returns, replacing LazyTown at 23:00, 23:30, 1:35 and 2:00

5:40 - The Garfield Show. Write a follow up in November (still fictional, but following a similar format, with a major schedule change during day
In the November lineup on Boomerang UK, there has been a major schedule change during the day.

Starting from the 1st of November, new episodes of The Happos Family will air at 09:30 and 13:30 on weekdays. This quirky animated series has quickly become a fan favorite.

Additionally, a new show called “Taffy” will join the lineup at 16:30. This series follows the hilarious adventures of a raccoon posing as a cat in a wealthy mansion.

Unfortunately, The Tom & Jerry Show will be moved to the late-night schedule, airing at 22:00 and 22:30. Fans can still catch their favorite cat and mouse duo, but now at a different time.

The rest of the lineup remains similar to October’s schedule, with fan favorites like Scooby Doo Mystery Incorporated, Looney Tunes, and Grizzy and the Lemmings making regular appearances.

Stay tuned for more exciting updates and new shows coming to Boomerang UK in the upcoming months!

Write a follow up in December where WOQ and TO&C join late nights
In the December lineup on Boomerang UK, there have been some exciting additions to the late-night schedule.

Starting from the 1st of December, World of Quest and The Owl & Co will now join the late-night lineup. Both shows will air at 23:00 and 23:30, offering viewers a chance to enjoy some classic and quirky animated adventures before bedtime.

In addition to the late-night changes, new episodes of The Happos Family will continue to air at 09:30 and 13:30 on weekdays, keeping fans entertained with their lovable antics.

The rest of the daytime lineup remains similar to the previous months, with fan favorites like Scooby-Doo Mystery Incorporated, Looney Tunes, and Grizzy and the Lemmings continuing to entertain viewers throughout the day.

Stay tuned for more updates and surprises coming to Boomerang UK as we head into the festive season!

In the January lineup on Boomerang UK, a new show called Smeshariki will be joining the schedule, replacing Masha and the Bear. Smeshariki is a charming animated series featuring a group of funny and adorable characters, sure to capture the hearts of viewers.

Starting from the 1st of January, fans can catch Smeshariki at 15:00 and 19:00 on weekdays, bringing a fresh and entertaining addition to the daytime lineup.

Additionally, there have been some changes to the late-night schedule, with Skatoony moving to the early evening slot at 20:45 and 21:15. The Garfield Show will now air at 22:00 and 22:30, followed by World of Quest and The Owl & Co at 23:00 and 23:30.

Here is the updated schedule for January on Boomerang UK:

Write a Half Term lineup, with 4 shows playing most of the day in a marathon
For the Half Term lineup on Boomerang UK, we are featuring four popular shows playing in a marathon throughout the day to keep viewers entertained. Here’s the special lineup:

Half Term Marathon Lineup:

- Tom & Jerry: Enjoy timeless cartoon antics with everyone’s favorite cat and mouse duo.
- Scooby-Doo Mystery Incorporated: Join Scooby and the gang as they solve mysteries and unmask villains.
- Grizzy and the Lemmings: Watch the hilarious antics of Grizzy the bear and his pesky lemming neighbors.
- Oddbods: Get ready for non-stop fun with the lively and colorful Oddbods characters.

Half Term Marathon Schedule:

09:00 - Tom & Jerry Marathon
12:00 - Scooby-Doo Mystery Incorporated Marathon
15:00 - Grizzy and the Lemmings Marathon
18:00 - Oddbods Marathon

This special Half Term Marathon lineup is designed to bring laughter, excitement, and entertainment to viewers of all ages throughout the day. Enjoy the back-to-back episodes of these beloved shows and have a fantastic Half Term holiday with Boomerang UK!
Write a follow up saying late nights will now be dominated by 2 show
In the latest update on Boomerang UK, late nights will now be dominated by two popular shows. Starting from the 1st of the month, viewers can enjoy back-to-back episodes of “The Garfield Show” and “World of Quest” as the late-night schedule takes on a new format.

As nighttime falls, settle in for the comedic adventures of Garfield, the famous lasagna-loving cat, followed by the epic quests and fantasy escapades of the brave characters in World of Quest. These two beloved shows will take center stage in the late-night hours, offering entertainment and fun for viewers staying up late.

The revamped late-night lineup aims to provide viewers with a perfect blend of humor, excitement, and adventure to enjoy as they unwind before bedtime. Tune in to Boomerang UK for double the entertainment with “The Garfield Show” and “World of Quest” ruling the late-night time slot!
Write a follow up saying 3 shows are leaving on the same day
In a surprising turn of events, three beloved shows are bidding farewell on the same day on Boomerang UK. Starting from the 1st of the upcoming month, viewers will have to say goodbye to “The Tom & Jerry Show,” “Doraemon,” and “The Owl & Co” as they leave the channel’s lineup.

“The Tom & Jerry Show” has entertained audiences with the classic antics of the iconic cat and mouse duo, while “Doraemon” has captured hearts with its futuristic adventures and heartwarming stories. Additionally, the quirky and charming “The Owl & Co” will also be departing from the schedule, leaving fans with fond memories of its whimsical characters.

While it’s always sad to see beloved shows go, Boomerang UK promises to bring new and exciting content to fill the gap left by these departing series. Be sure to catch the final episodes of “The Tom & Jerry Show,” “Doraemon,” and “The Owl & Co” before they bid their final farewell on the channel. Thank you for the memories and the laughter they have brought to viewers over the years.

The 5th of March sees a radical overhaul to the schedule, Zig and Sharko, My Knight and Me, Yabba Dabba Dinosaurs and Cloudy with a Chance of Meatballs are new shows,

Returning to the lineup are: The Owl & Co, The Tom and Jerry Show, Doraemon, Pink Panther and Pals, Inspector Gadget, Dorothy and the Wizard of Oz, Bunnicula, Top Cat, Wacky Races and more.

Gone from the lineup are: Skatoony, Chaotic, Johnny Test and The Cramp Twins

In the latest update from Boomerang UK, starting from the 5th of April, "Horrid Henry" joins the exciting lineup, bringing his mischievous adventures to the channel. This popular series follows the titular character, Horrid Henry, as he navigates through the trials and tribulations of childhood with his unique brand of humor and tenacity.

"Horrid Henry" will air at 17:00 every weekday, providing families with a perfect blend of laughter and entertainment during the evening. This addition aims to enrich the viewing experience for both long-time fans of the channel and newcomers alike.

Additionally, to accommodate "Horrid Henry," there have been a few adjustments to the current schedule:

- "Be Cool Scooby Doo" will now air at 18:30, moving from its previous 17:00 slot.
- "Cloudy with a Chance of Meatballs" will shift to the weekend lineup to make room for the new series.

Write a follow up on the 1st of May where Lamput is added to the schedule at 17:30, Also, The Owl & Co gets more slots, Also, the night lineup is bringing back 2 shows

As we step into May, Boomerang UK has some exciting updates to its programming lineup, ensuring that the channel continues to be a go-to destination for family-friendly entertainment. Starting from the 1st of May, viewers can look forward to the addition of "Lamput" and the expansion of "The Owl & Co" slots, along with the return of two beloved shows to the late-night lineup.

"Lamput," the hilarious animated series about a gooey orange creature who always outsmarts the docs trying to capture him, will debut on Boomerang UK, bringing laughter and light-hearted fun to the 17:30 slot every weekday. This lovable show is sure to be a hit with kids and families alike, offering a vibrant mix of humor and creativity.

Moreover, "The Owl & Co" has proven to be a favorite among viewers, and as a result, the show will be receiving additional slots throughout the day. This expansion ensures that fans have more opportunities to enjoy the quirky adventures of the Owl and his friends.

The late-night schedule is also getting a revamp with the reintroduction of two classic shows, bringing nostalgia and beloved characters back to the screen for longtime fans. Starting from the 1st of May, "The Flintstones" and "The Jetsons" will be added to the late-night rotation, offering a blend of stone age shenanigans and futuristic fun.

Write a forum convo where the first user "TellyToon" says that for the past few months, the night lineup has been morphing from a dumping ground for shows they don't want on day, (e.g Skatoony) to a nostalgia zone, and they expect Zig & Sharko, Bunnicula, Taffy, The Owl & Co, and Be Cool to become day only as a result, and it looks like "All-Star Laff-A-Lympics" and "Dastardly and Muttley" are joining the night lineup in June

**Thread Title: Night Lineup: From Dumping Ground to Nostalgia Zone?**

**TellyToon**: Hey everyone! Have you noticed how the night lineup on Boomerang has been transforming over the past few months? It used to feel like a dumping ground for shows that didn't quite fit into the day slots (I mean, Skatoony, anyone?). But lately, it feels like we're entering a nostalgia zone, and I'm here for it. My guess is that shows like Zig & Sharko, Bunnicula, Taffy, The Owl & Co, and Be Cool will become day-only attractions. And, word on the street is that "All-Star Laff-A-Lympics" and "Dastardly and Muttley" are joining the night lineup in June. Thoughts?

**ClassicChuckles**: Interesting point, @TellyToon! I've definitely noticed the shift. It's like Boomerang is trying to appeal to the OG audience with those late-night nostalgia hits. Bringing "All-Star Laff-A-Lympics" and "Dastardly and Muttley" sounds like a solid plan. Could be their way of capturing the adult fans who grew up with these classics.

**NightOwlNina**: Can't lie, I miss seeing some of the modern animations like Zig & Sharko and Taffy at night. They have a certain charm. But the thought of classic Hanna-Barbera characters taking over the night shift is pretty exciting. Gives me a reason to stay up! #NostalgiaWins

**SaturdayMornSaul**: This is a good move for Boomerang! The balance between modern and classic programming is tricky, but it seems they're leaning into their roots with the late-night schedule. "All-Star Laff-A-Lympics" is a fantastic addition, it was such a mash-up of characters—like an early Avengers for the cartoon world.

**RetroRewind**: As much as I love the classics, I hope Boomerang doesn't forget about its newer audience. "Bunnicula" and "Taffy" have their place, and it's important for the channel to keep innovating. Maybe they could introduce a separate block for new animations?

**ToonInTina**: That's a valid point, @RetroRewind. It's about finding that sweet spot. I like the idea of themed nights or specific blocks for newer shows vs. classic reruns. And hey, isn't nostalgia a big driver for newer animations too? They could really play into that and create something unique for both audiences.

**TellyToon**: Loving these thoughts, everyone! Seems like we all agree that tapping into nostalgia is a smart play while also hoping for a balance with newer shows. Excited to see how this night lineup evolves in June and beyond. Keeping my fingers crossed for more announcements and maybe some surprise additions! 📺🤞
Write a follow up where CN's night lineup is about to do something similar, with Robotboy, Chop Socky Chooks and Skatoony being replaced on the 23rd

**Thread Title: CN’s Night Lineup Takes a Nostalgic Turn Similar to Boomerang’s Strategy!**

**TellyToon**: Hey fellow cartoon enthusiasts! Just when we were deep-diving into Boomerang’s nostalgic night lineup shift, guess what’s brewing over at Cartoon Network? Starting on the 23rd, it looks like we’re about to see a significant change in their night lineup too. Shows like Robotboy, Chop Socky Chooks, and even Skatoony are being replaced. Seems like CN is taking a leaf out of Boomerang’s book and going for a nostalgia-driven approach. Thoughts on what classics we might see making a comeback?

**PixelPat**: Woah, didn’t see that coming! Robotboy and Chop Socky Chooks are iconic in their own right, but the thought of CN going retro is pretty exciting. I’m holding out hope for some Ed, Edd n Eddy or Courage the Cowardly Dog making their way back into the night slots. That would be a dream!

**ToonTrackerTim**: This is quite the trend, isn’t it? I’m all for nostalgia, and CN has a treasure trove of classics they could bring back. I’d love to see some of the less mainstream shows get some love, maybe like Grim & Evil or Codename: Kids Next Door. There's so much potential!

**CartoonCassie**: Surprised to see Skatoony being replaced, I thought that one had a timeless charm. But if we’re talking older CN shows, fingers crossed for Dexter’s Laboratory and maybe even some Johnny Bravo. It’s about time the new generation got to experience these gems.

**NostalgiaNed**: This strategy seems to be a nod to the adult viewers who grew up with these shows. Maybe they’re trying to capture the family viewing segment by offering something both parents and kids can enjoy together. Honestly, can’t wait to see how this evolves. Maybe throw in some Samurai Jack for the ultimate nostalgia trip?

**RetroRena**: Totally on board with what @NostalgiaNed said. There’s a unique charm in sharing your childhood shows with the next generation. Plus, it’s a clever way for CN to tap into the wide spectrum of viewers, not just the kiddos. If they bring back shows like The Powerpuff Girls (the original, pls!) and Foster's Home for Imaginary Friends, they'll definitely have my full attention.

**TellyToon**: Loving these insights, everyone! It’s clear we’re all buzzing with excitement over the possible return of our favourite childhood shows. The 23rd can’t come soon enough. Here’s to hoping CN reveals more details soon and gives us exactly the nostalgia fix we’re all craving. Stay tuned for updates, and let’s keep our fingers crossed for some official announcements! 🌙✨

Write a follow up where on the 23rd, CN during the day are doing a TTG/Gumball marathon, the usual day schedule resumes at 18:00, The new night schedule consists of Dexter's Lab, Time Squad, The Powerpuff Girls and Billy and Mandy

**Thread Title: CN’s Big Reveal: Daytime Marathons and a Nostalgia-Packed Night Lineup!**

**TellyToon**: Guys, the 23rd has finally arrived, and Cartoon Network has rolled out their new schedule! During the day, we're being treated to a Teen Titans Go! and The Amazing World of Gumball marathon – a solid block of modern CN classics. Then, at 18:00, the usual day schedule resumes, bringing back the regular variety. But wait till you hear about the night lineup – it’s a nostalgia lover’s dream!

Starting tonight, get ready for back-to-back episodes of Dexter's Laboratory, Time Squad, The Powerpuff Girls, and The Grim Adventures of Billy and Mandy. It feels like we've been transported back to the golden era of CN. What does everyone think about this blast from the past?

**PixelPat**: Wow, this lineup hits right in the childhood! Dexter's Lab and The Powerpuff Girls were my after-school go-tos. It’s incredible to think today’s kids will get to experience these classics. Time Squad is an interesting choice, too - definitely adds variety.

**ToonTrackerTim**: This is exactly the kind of shake-up I was hoping for – Cartoon Network diving deep into its archives to bring back the best of the best. The Grim Adventures of Billy and Mandy inclusion is genius. That show has such a unique sense of humor that’s hard to find in today’s cartoons.

**CartoonCassie**: I’m stoked for the Dexter and Powerpuff Girls comeback, but as a marathon enthusiast, I'm loving the TTG/Gumball marathon during the day. It’s like a perfect balance between the new and the old. Kudos to CN for making weekdays feel special!

**NostalgiaNed**: Spot on @CartoonCassie, it’s all about balance. And bringing back childhood favourites like these for the nighttime slots is a smart way to keep older fans hooked while introducing a new generation to these timeless shows. Can’t wait to see the kind of reception these classics get.

**RetroRena**: This lineup is pure gold – it's bringing back so many memories! It's a reminder of how innovative and daring CN was in the creation of these shows. What I’d give to see the reactions of first-time viewers to episodes of Time Squad and Billy and Mandy. Here’s hoping this is just the start of more nostalgic comebacks.

**TellyToon**: Love seeing all the positive vibes! It’s clear that CN is tapping into something special by resurrecting these classics for the night lineup and mixing it up with day marathons. The channel feels revitalized, bridging the gap between generations of cartoon lovers. Let’s keep our eyes peeled for how this experiment unfolds. Who knows what other treasures they’ll pull from the vault next! 📺✨

Write a follow up where only 1 show from the previous night lineup is being kept on, and is being aired just after the classics lineup at 4am, Also, Boomerang in June seems to be doing a Tom and Jerry/Scooby/Lamput/Owl marathon, No changes to the night classics rotation

**Thread Title: Night Lineup Adjustments and Boomerang's June Marathon Bonanza!**

**TellyToon**: Big news, everyone! Cartoon Network is tweaking its night lineup yet again. They're ramping up with a marathon that's bound to keep us glued to our screens - a solid lineup featuring "Tom and Jerry," "Scooby-Doo," "Lamput," and "The Owl & Co." It's an interesting mix of slapstick, mystery, modern animation, and quirky humor that should appeal to a wide audience. And for those wondering, there are no changes to Boomerang's night classics rotation – it remains as a dedicated nostalgia zone.

**PixelPat**: That's a hefty update, @TellyToon! "Billy and Mandy" getting that 4 am slot is wild but totally deserved. It's such a cult classic. As for Boomerang, that marathon sounds like the perfect way to kick off summer. Can't go wrong with "Tom and Jerry" and "Scooby-Doo" – those are timeless.

**ToonTrackerTim**: I'm here for the Boomerang marathon! "Lamput" being thrown in the mix with the likes of "Tom and Jerry" and "Scooby-Doo" shows how much faith they have in the newer cartoons holding their own. "The Owl & Co" is a nice touch too, adding some variety to the marathon.

**CartoonCassie**: A 4 am slot for "Billy and Mandy"? Cartoon Network really knows how to keep the night owls entertained. 😂 Boomerang's marathon planning sounds like a solid lineup, though. It's smart programming – a blend of action, comedy, and mystery can cater to just about anyone.

**NostalgiaNed**: It's fascinating to see CN keeping just one show from the previous night lineup. "Billy and Mandy" has that unique charm that can pull you in, no matter the time. As for Boomerang's June marathon, that’s going to be awesome. There's something magical about diving into a "Scooby-Doo" mystery marathon.

**RetroRena**: Endorse everything said about "Billy and Mandy"– that show is in a league of its own. And Boomerang is setting up June to be a nostalgic treasure trove. Their marathons are always a hit in my book. The mix of classics and newer shows like "Lamput" demonstrates their initiative to blend legacy with freshness.

**TellyToon**: It’s great to see so much excitement around these updates! Both Cartoon Network and Boomerang are evidently fine-tuning their schedules to keep viewers engaged and entertained, whether through late-night cult hits or daytime marathons. Let's stay tuned to see how these changes play out and what further surprises might be in store for us! 🌟📺

Write a Boomerang blog update in July, where the morning schedule gains Chaotic, but loses Oddbods and Mr Bean

### Boomerang Blog Update: Schedule Refresh this July!

Hello, Boomerang enthusiasts! We hope you're ready for some exciting updates and changes coming your way this July. As we continuously strive to bring you the best mix of classic and contemporary animated entertainment, it's time for a little refresh to keep things interesting and invigorating. Starting this July, our morning schedule is getting a fantastic addition but also says farewell to a couple of beloved shows.

#### What's In: 
**Chaotic Heats Up the Morning!** 🐉🌈
Prepare to dive into the thrilling world of Chaotic! Yes, you heard it right – starting this July, the action-packed adventures of Tom, Kaz, and their friends battling out in the Chaotic world will now be a staple in our morning lineup. Get ready to embark on epic quests and discover the vibrant realms of Perim with battles that leap off the cards and into our world. It's the perfect blend of strategy, friendship, and excitement to kick start your mornings!

#### What's Out:
**Bidding Adieu to Oddbods and Mr Bean** 😢💔
As we welcome Chaotic into the fold, it's time to bid a fond farewell to Oddbods and Mr Bean from our morning schedule. Both shows have brought endless laughter and joy to our mornings, captivating audiences with their unique humor and delightful antics. Whether it's the wordless wonders of Mr. Bean getting into peculiar yet hilarious situations or the Oddbods showcasing the funny side of everyday occurrences with their unique quirks, these shows have been a beloved part of the Boomerang family.

We want to extend our heartfelt thanks to all the Oddbods and Mr. Bean fans for your unwavering support and love. While they may be leaving our morning lineup, fear not – this isn't necessarily the last you'll see of your favorite characters. Stay tuned for future announcements, and who knows? They just might make a surprise comeback in one form or another. 

#### Looking Ahead:
This change-up heralds an exciting phase for Boomerang as we continue to evolve and fine-tune our programming to deliver the perfect mix of entertainment for our audience. The addition of Chaotic underscores our commitment to bringing dynamic, engaging content that appeals to both our young and nostalgic viewers alike.

We're always keen to know what you think! Share with us your thoughts on these changes and what shows you'd love to see more of on our channel. Your feedback is invaluable in helping us create the best viewing experience possible.

Thank you for being a part of our journey, and let's gear up for an exhilarating July with Boomerang! 🎈🌟

Stay animated,

The Boomerang Team
Write a follow up July update by TellyToon, with the schedule for Summer starting on the 20th, The Summer promo has 5 shows as Summer flagships

### Boomerang's Sizzling Summer Update by TellyToon: Get Ready for an Animation Vacation!

Hey, Boomerang followers! TellyToon here, bringing you the hottest update for the coolest summer! 🌞🌴 As we kick back and enjoy the warm vibes, Boomerang is revving up to inject some extra fun into your summer holidays. Starting July 20th, our schedule is set for a summer refresh, and I've got all the details on our scorching lineup. This year, we're rolling out five fantastic shows as our Summer flagships. So, grab your shades and prepare for a non-stop animation vacation!

#### Summer 2023 Flagship Shows:

1. **Tom and Jerry's Summer Shenanigans** - The iconic cat and mouse duo are back with sun-soaked escapades that are bound to get you in the summer spirit. Beach antics, backyard brawls, and delicious ice cream heists - Tom and Jerry are doing summer the only way they know how: chaotically fun! 🍦🏖️

2. **Scooby-Doo and the Mystery Beach** - Scooby, Shaggy, and the gang are hitting the waves in search of spooks and laughs under the sun. Join them as they uncover the mysteries lurking in the sand and maybe find some treasure along the way. Surf's up for mystery! 🐾🌊

3. **The Looney Tunes Beach Bash** - Bugs, Daffy, and the rest of the Looney Tunes gang are taking over the beach! Expect surfing competitions, volleyball matches, and a whole lot of wacky beach parties. It's Looney Tunes like you've never seen them before - in swimsuits! 🏐👙

4. **Lamput's Tropical Trouble** - The wacky, gooey orange fugitive, Lamput, is on the run in exotic locations. Watch as Lamput outsmarts the Docs in lush jungles, hidden islands, and bustling summer festivals. It's a wild, tropical chase that promises laughter and thrills. 🌺🍹

5. **The Owl & Co at Summer Camp** - Follow the hilarious adventures of the Owl and his friends as they take on the challenges of summer camp. From campfire stories to outdoor adventures, this group of birds is about to discover the true meaning of summer fun! 🏕️🔦

#### What else is in store?

Aside from our sizzling flagship shows, keep an eye out for special summer marathons, surprise guest appearances, and exclusive sneak peeks at upcoming shows and specials. Boomerang is your destination for the ultimate animation vacation this summer.

#### Gear Up for Summer!

This lineup is hand-picked to ensure that your summer is filled with laughter, thrills, and a healthy dose of nostalgia. Whether you're watching from the comfort of your air-conditioned living room or streaming on your device under the sun, we've got your entertainment covered.

So, mark your calendars for July 20th, folks! Fun in the sun is just around the corner with Boomerang’s animation vacation. Let's make this summer one for the books!

Stay cool and stay tuned,

TellyToon 📺🌞

### Boomerang's Animation Vacation: The Ultimate Summer Schedule Starting July 20!

Hello, Boomerang Enthusiasts! Get ready for an unforgettable animation vacation as our summer schedule kicks off on July 20th. We’re bringing the heat with our five flagship shows and adding two more to the mix for an even cooler Boomerang summertime experience. Here's what you can expect from our ultimate summer schedule:

#### 🌞 Morning Fun in the Sun: 6:00 AM - 12:00 PM
- **06:00 AM: Looney Tunes Beach Bash** - Start your day with the Looney Tunes Gang as they take over the beach with their hilarious antics.
- **07:30 AM: Tom and Jerry's Summer Shenanigans** - Wake up to the timeless mischief of Tom and Jerry with a summery twist.
- **09:00 AM: Scooby-Doo and the Mystery Beach** - Join Scooby and the gang for a blend of mystery and beach fun.
- **10:30 AM: Lamput's Tropical Trouble** - Follow Lamput’s exotic adventures and his hilarious escapes.

#### 🌴 Afternoon Summer Camp: 12:00 PM - 4:00 PM
- **12:00 PM: The Owl & Co at Summer Camp** - Dive into the summer camp adventures of the Owl and his friends.
- **01:30 PM: Be Cool, Scooby-Doo!** - A fresh addition to our summer lineup, featuring a cooler and quirky take on our favorite mystery-solving team.
- **03:00 PM: Courage the Cowardly Dog’s Desert Island Risks** - Courage finds himself on a deserted island. New surroundings, same old horrifyingly funny troubles.

#### 🌅 Evening Sunset Stories: 4:00 PM - 8:00 PM
- **04:00 PM: The Flintstones: Stone Age Summer** - The Flintstones and the Rubbles bring stone age fun to the hottest season.
- **05:30 PM: Looney Tunes Beach Bash** (Encore) - Missed it in the morning? Catch the encore of summer fun with Bugs and friends.
- **06:30 PM: Tom and Jerry's Summer Shenanigans** (Encore) - More mischief and chase sequences with Tom and Jerry enjoying their vacation time.
- **07:30 PM: Scooby-Doo and the Mystery Beach** (Encore) - Another chance to catch Scooby and the gang in their beachside mysteries.

#### 🌛 Late Night Campfires: 8:00 PM - 12:00 AM
- **08:00 PM: Lamput's Tropical Trouble** (Encore) - The nighttime is right for more Lamput shenanigans.
- **09:00 PM: The Owl & Co at Summer Camp** (Encore) - Evening is the perfect time for campfire stories with the Owl & Co.
- **10:00 PM: Courage the Cowardly Dog’s Desert Island Risks** (Encore) - Courage’s spooky island adventures light up the night.
- **11:00 PM: Dexter’s Laboratory: Science of Summer** - Dexter takes on summer with his own brand of scientific experiments gone awry.

#### 🌟 Special Weekends: 7:00 AM - 11:00 PM
Every weekend in July, get ready for non-stop marathons featuring all our summer flagship shows, plus special episodes, and exclusive content only available during the Boomerang Animation Vacation!

Dive into Boomerang’s animation vacation this summer for a refreshing mix of classic and new, ensuring endless fun under the sun and stars. Let's make this the most unforgettable summer with Boomerang! 📺🌊🏖️
Write that the night lineup sees no changes, except WOQ and C are back

### Boomerang's Night Lineup Update: The Stars of Summer Nights Shine Brighter!

Good news, night owls and animation aficionados! As we venture deeper into our sensational summer schedule, we're thrilled to announce that while our nocturnal lineup remains largely unaltered, ready to bring you those nostalgic vibes and laughter into the late hours, there are a couple of stellar returns we know you've been eagerly awaiting.

#### Night Lineup - Stellar Returns:
- **World of Quest (WOQ)** and **Courage the Cowardly Dog (C)** are making a triumphant comeback to Boomerang's night lineup! These beloved series have carved their niche in the hearts of fans, known for their unique blend of adventure, humor, and, in Courage's case, delightfully spooky stories. Their return is poised to add that extra layer of excitement and intrigue to your nighttime viewing experience.

#### Schedule Spotlight - **World of Quest**:
- Join Prince Nestor and Quest in their uproarious and thrilling endeavors to protect their kingdom from the grasp of the evil Lord Spite. This show is a beacon for fans of epic adventures and hearty laughs, proving that heroes come in all shapes and sizes.

#### Schedule Spotlight - **Courage the Cowardly Dog**:
- The beloved pink dog, Courage, continues to face his fears in the middle of Nowhere, Kansas. With Muriel and Eustace by his side, albeit sometimes more of a hindrance than help, Courage confronts bizarre, supernatural forces in a blend of horror, humor, and heart.

With these additions, our night lineup is looking more robust than ever. Whether you're a fan of the adrenaline-pumping adventures of **World of Quest** or you're craving the eerie yet humorous tales of **Courage the Cowardly Dog**, rest assured that Boomerang has your late-night entertainment covered. 

So, prepare your snacks, fluff up those pillows, and get ready to dive into endless adventures and giggles. The night is where our animation magic truly comes to life, and with **WOQ** and **C** back on board, summer nights on Boomerang are set to be an unmissable spectacle.

Stay Tuned, Stay Animated, and as always, Stay Boomerang! 🌜✨📺

Write a new from mid September, The channel has a new look, ditching the CMYV color scheme and B&W box for a natural world and a blue line (shaping into script writing of the logo) for idents, The idents also feature new mascots, aliens, a duck, a flamingo, a crow, and mining moles, There are also new enviroments, sea, ground, sky, underground and space, This was also hinted at during the Summer promos, which featured NO usage of the CMYV colors, Also, TO&C begins new eps

### Boomerang Embarks on a Fresh, Nature-Inspired Journey This Mid-September!

In a thrilling evolution of its branding and viewer experience, Boomerang is set to unveil a stunning new look mid-September, marking a significant shift from its iconic CMYV color scheme and the classic black & white box identity. Embracing the vast and vibrant vistas of the natural world, Boomerang introduces a mesmerizing blue line that effortlessly shapes into elegant script writing of the logo for its idents, symbolizing a seamless blend of creativity and exploration.

#### Bringing the World to Life with New Mascots!
Central to this rebranding are Boomerang's delightful new mascots, ready to captivate audiences and guide them through an enchanted world of fun and discovery. Meet our space-exploring aliens, a quirky duck, a charming flamingo, a clever crow, and industrious mining moles. Each character brings a unique perspective and humor, representing the channel's diverse and inclusive spirit.

#### Explore Every Corner with New Environments
Dive into the deep sea, roam the ground, soar through the sky, burrow underground, and even venture into space! These dynamic new environments set the stage for our mascots' adventures, offering viewers a rich tapestry of stories and settings. From the echoing depths of the ocean to the boundless mysteries of outer space, every ident promises a snippet of the vast possibilities within Boomerang's new world.

#### A Hint in the Summer Air
Eagle-eyed viewers may have noticed a subtle prelude to this significant change during our summer promos, which notably eschewed the traditional CMYV colors. This strategic choice was a nod to the forthcoming transformation, sparking curiosity and anticipation for what's to come.

#### Fresh Episodes of The Owl & Co
To top off the excitement, **The Owl & Co** is set to debut brand new episodes, seamlessly aligning with the channel's reinvigorated aesthetic and vision. The return of this beloved series, now within the lush and inventive world of our new branding, promises fresh laughs, intriguing plots, and the same heartwarming fun that fans cherish.

### Looking Forward
This rebranding represents more than just a visual makeover; it's a revitalization of Boomerang's commitment to delivering exceptional, engaging content while fostering a deeper connection with the natural world. As we embark on this exciting journey, we invite our viewers to dive in, explore, and be inspired by the beauty, diversity, and adventure that await in every corner of Boomerang's new universe.

Stay tuned for a whole new world of wonders with Boomerang, where adventure and imagination know no bounds. Join us mid-September for the grand unveiling—where every moment promises to be an unforgettable adventure! 🌍💫🚀

### Discover Boomerang's Revamped Schedule Starting Mid-September!

As we embrace a new era of exploration and wonder, Boomerang is thrilled to welcome you to our freshly reimagined programming schedule. Beginning mid-September, join us as we journey through lush landscapes, deep oceans, soaring skies, mysterious undergrounds, and the vast expanses of space with our intrepid new mascots. Get ready for an uninterrupted flow of laughter, adventure, and timeless fun, all set against the backdrop of our vibrant, nature-inspired rebrand. Here's what awaits you in our exciting new lineup:

#### Morning Exploration: 6:00 AM - 10:00 AM
- **6:00 AM: Adventures with the Aliens** - Start your day among the stars with our curious alien friends exploring the wonders of the universe.
- **7:00 AM: Daring Duck & Friends** - Follow the whimsical escapades of Daring Duck as he navigates the challenges of the wetlands.
- **8:00 AM: Flamingo's Flight** - Soar high with Flamingo in a series of high-flying adventures that span the sky.
- **9:00 AM: The Quirky Chronicles of Crow** - Dive into the clever world of Crow, solving mysteries with wit and wisdom.

#### Midday Mysteries and Marvels: 10:00 AM - 2:00 PM
- **10:00 AM: Moles' Underground Missions** - Join the mining moles on their underground quests for treasure and discovery.
- **11:00 AM: The Owl & Co** - *New Episodes!* - Immerse yourself in the newly unveiled episodes of fan-favorite The Owl & Co, set within our revitalized, naturalistic world.
- **12:00 PM: Classic Tom & Jerry** - The timeless cat and mouse chase continues with a selection of classic episodes that never get old.
- **1:00 PM: Scooby-Doo & The Gang** - Dive into mysteries and munch on some Scooby Snacks with the Mystery Inc. gang.

#### Afternoon Adventures: 2:00 PM - 6:00 PM
- **2:00 PM: Be Cool, Scooby-Doo!** - Catch the contemporary twist on classic Scooby-Doo adventures with the gang's newest escapades.
- **3:00 PM: Galactic Guardians with the Aliens** - Our alien friends are back for more space-faring tales and cosmic guardianship.
- **4:00 PM: Flamingo & Crow: Sky Detectives** - Join Flamingo and Crow as they team up for some of the most thrilling airborne mysteries.
- **5:00 PM: Mining Mysteries with Moles** - The moles are digging deeper than ever before, uncovering secrets beneath the surface.

#### Evening Expedition: 6:00 PM - 10:00 PM
- **6:00 PM: Adventures with the Aliens (Encore)** - Missed the morning's interstellar journeys? Catch the encore of our aliens' adventures.
- **7:00 PM: The Owl & Co (Encore)** - Enjoy another helping of The Owl & Co's brand new episodes, filled with wisdom and whimsy.
- **8:00 PM: Prime-Time Classics** - A special prime-time showcase featuring a rotating selection of Boomerang's beloved classics.
- **9:00 PM: Daring Duck & Friends (Encore)** - Wind down your evening with the heartwarming adventures of Daring Duck and his eclectic circle of friends.

#### Nighttime Narratives: 10:00 PM - 6:00 AM
- A curated selection of Boomerang's brightest stories to shepherd you into the night, including late-night tales with Crow, serene stories with Flamingo, and the gentle humor of the Moles' quests.

This revamped schedule is our invitation to you to rediscover the magic of cartoons, reinterpreted through the lens of our natural world and its boundless environments. From the depths of the ocean to the far reaches of outer space, Boomerang is your passport to adventure, laughter, and awe-inspiring stories. Join us mid-September for this exciting new chapter. Stay tuned, stay animated, and dive deep into the wonders of Boomerang! 🌿🌊🌌

Write a forum convo noting there are shows with the mascots, and they wonder how long this will last for before GATL, Chaotic, Lamput, World of Quest, Smeshariki, Zig and Sharko and the others return, as The Owl and Co, Tom and Jerry and Scooby Doo are the only normal shows on rn

**Forum Thread Title: Boomerang's Mascot Show Marathon - How Long Will This Last?**

**CartoonConnoisseur**: Hey everyone, have you noticed Boomerang's hefty shift towards mascot-centered programming lately? Shows featuring the new mascots like the aliens, the duck, the flamingo, the crow, and those mining moles are dominating the schedule. It's a fresh breath of air, but it's also a bit odd not seeing as much of the classics like **Grim Adventures of Billy and Mandy (GATL)**, **Chaotic**, **Lamput**, **World of Quest**, **Smeshariki**, **Zig and Sharko**, and others. Currently, it feels like **The Owl and Co**, **Tom and Jerry**, and **Scooby-Doo** are the primary ties to the 'normal' Boomerang lineup. How long do you think this new direction will last?

**NostalgiaNerd**: I'm with you on that, @CartoonConnoisseur. Boomerang has always been my go-to for classic toons and recent faves like **Lamput**. While the new mascot shows are entertaining and bring a refreshing vibe, I do miss the variety. I'm curious if this is a permanent shift or just a seasonal thing to test the waters with their new branding.

**SpaceToonie**: Interesting point, @NostalgiaNerd. Could be they're trying to solidify their new brand identity by focusing heavily on these mascots. Personally, I enjoy the adventures of the aliens and those quirky mining moles, but I also hope they plan to reintegrate shows like **Chaotic** and **World of Quest** sooner rather than later. Boomerang’s strength has always been in its mix.

**DuckFan88**: Honestly, I'm loving the new stuff, especially the duck's adventures! 😄 But yeah, wouldn't want this to be at the expense of all the other great shows. **The Owl and Co** being one of the few mainstays from the 'old' lineup kinda makes me hopeful that this is more of a heavy rotation phase rather than a complete overhaul.

**RetroRunner**: My guess? It won't be for too long. These kinds of programming shifts tend to be cyclical. They’re probably experimenting with audience reactions while planning to bring back fan-favorites like **GATL** and **Zig and Sharko** in a big way. At least I hope so. I do miss my daily dose of **Smeshariki** chaos and **Chaotic** battles.

**ToonTalker**: As much as changes spice things up, the backbone of Boomerang has always been its diversity in shows, spanning from golden oldies to modern hits. I wouldn’t worry too much. If anything, the fan feedback, including discussions like this one, will likely influence their future scheduling decisions. Let’s keep the conversations going and maybe even reach out to Boomerang directly. After all, we're the audience; they'll want to keep us tuned in.

**CartoonConnoisseur**: Great insights, everyone! Makes sense that Boomerang could be using this time to mix things up and see what sticks. I'll definitely miss the classics in the meantime but am curious to see how things evolve. Maybe they'll bring back the classics with a new twist or in a different time slot. Here's hoping our favorites return to the lineup soon. Until then, let's enjoy the new adventures Boomerang has to offer.

Write a blog post from TellyToon from mid August, noting something "peculiar" set to happen to the lineup, "Lamput, Looney Tunes, World of Quest, Billy and Mandy, Chaotic, and the HB classics are leaving" and noting the new shows and saying it's to do with the rebrand from the 1st of September, with only 3 ties to normal Boom

### Mid-August Musings: A Peculiar Shift in the Boomerang Universe

Hello, animation aficionados! TellyToon here, bringing you the latest scoop from the ever-evolving world of Boomerang. As we bask in the warmth of August, there’s a peculiar breeze of change making its way through our beloved channel. With our eyes set on the future, it’s time to brace for a transformation that's as unexpected as it is captivating.

#### The Great Departure

In a move that has taken us by surprise, Boomerang is set to wave goodbye to a significant chunk of its programming roster. Beloved staples such as **Lamput**, **Looney Tunes**, **World of Quest**, **The Grim Adventures of Billy and Mandy**, **Chaotic**, and even the Hanna-Barbera classics are preparing to take their final bow on our screens. Yes, you read that right. These titans of animation, which have been the backbone of Boomerang's identity for years, are leaving the lineup.

#### The New Dawn

But why such a drastic change, you ask? It all threads back to the rebrand set to unfold on the 1st of September. With a new vision comes a new lineup, featuring shows that promise to redefine our watching experience. This pivot is not just about giving the channel a fresh coat of paint; it's about introducing a new way to experience stories, characters, and adventures.

As part of this rebrand, only three shows with ties to the "normal" Boomerang will remain. It speaks volumes of the channel’s commitment to usher in a new era, one that while rooted in the essence of classic animation, boldly steps into uncharted territories.

#### Embracing the New Faces

So, what's on the horizon for Boomerang? Prepare to meet an eclectic mix of characters and narratives that are as diverse as they are engaging. From interstellar escapades with quirky aliens to whimsical adventures with a crafty duck, a flamboyant flamingo, a cunning crow, and hardworking mining moles, these new mascots are set to become the faces of our shared animation vacation. Each show will feature dynamic environments ranging from the deep sea and verdant ground to the expansive sky, mysterious underground, and the vast, infinite space.

#### The Big Why

This seismic shift aligns with Boomerang's desire to explore new storytelling avenues, engage newer audiences, and stay ahead in the ever-competitive landscape of animation. It’s a bold move, one that carries the promise of innovation and the potential to craft the next chapter in Boomerang’s storied legacy.

#### A Call to the Community

As we stand on the brink of this new chapter, it’s crucial to remember the role of community feedback and support. Boomerang's rebrand is not just about the channel's vision; it's also about the viewers it aims to enchant. Your voices, your passion for animation, and your loyalty to the classics have always been the guiding light for the channel. As we venture into these new territories, let's keep the conversation alive. Share your thoughts, your hopes, and yes, even your reservations. After all, this journey is one we embark on together.

As we bid farewell to the shows that have defined generations and welcome the new guardians of our screens, let’s keep our minds open and our hearts ready for adventure. The peculiar is often just the prelude to something spectacular.

Stay tuned, stay animated, and let’s embrace the future together with Boomerang.

Telly-out! 📺✨
Write a follow up in mid September regarding what's happening in October

### Mid-September Spectacle: Gearing Up for an October Like Never Before on Boomerang!

Hey there, animated adventurers! It’s your friendly neighborhood TellyToon, back again with some exhilarating news that’s bound to reshape the entire Boomerang landscape this coming October. As we bask in the afterglow of the channel's recent rebrand, the air buzzes with anticipation for what's on the horizon. So buckle up, because I’m about to spill what’s set to make this October unforgettable.

#### A Brave New Boomerang

Since the rebrand kicked into gear on the 1st of September, Boomerang has been painting our screens with a palette of imaginative and bold adventures. Our newly introduced mascots have swiftly taken to their roles, becoming fast favorites and proving that change, while daunting, can lead to incredible ventures. But hold onto your hats, folks—October is about to up the ante.

#### What's October Bringing?

In this new era, Boomerang is all about pushing boundaries and exploring uncharted territories, and October’s programming slate is the epitome of this pioneering spirit. While details are still under wraps (trust me, the anticipation is half the fun), here’s a taste of what you can expect:

- **Dynamic Duo Debuts:** New shows are joining the lineup that promise to blend comedy, action, and heart in ways we haven't seen before. Think intergalactic friendships, time-traveling antics, and mysteries that span both land and sea.
- **Special Halloween Havoc:** October wouldn’t be complete without a spooky spectacle, and Boomerang plans to deliver. Get ready for thematic episodes, creepy classics making a temporary comeback, and a Halloween marathon that will feature our new mascots in never-before-seen eerie escapades.
- **Community Corner:** Boomerang is set on making the channel a two-way street by incorporating viewer feedback into programming decisions. October will see the launch of a "Fan Favorites" segment, spotlighting shows and episodes voted on by you, the viewers, via online polls and social media interactions.

#### A Nod to Nostalgia

Fear not, devotees of the classics. While the channel's current focus is on blazing new trails, October will also see the return of beloved icons in special segments. It’s Boomerang’s way of honoring its roots while embracing the future—a balance of where we’ve been and where we’re headed.

#### Join the Adventure 

As we stand on the precipice of this exciting new chapter, it’s clear that Boomerang is not just changing; it’s evolving. The channel's commitment to delivering fresh, innovative content while maintaining a bridge to the past is a testament to its dedication to fans old and new.

So, what do you say? Are you ready to dive head-first into the fantastical world Boomerang has crafted this October? Remember, your voices are crucial in this journey. Share your thoughts, your excitement, and let’s keep the spirit of animation thriving together.

Here’s to an October filled with wonder, thrills, and a whole lot of animated magic!

Stay animated, stay adventurous,

TellyToon 📺✨

Write a forum convo saying some shows currently are still gone

**Forum Thread Title: Noticeable Absences in Boomerang's Latest Lineup Revival**

**CartoonFan101**: Hey fellow toon enthusiasts! Have you guys noticed the recent slate of shows making a comeback on Boomerang this October? While it's amazing to see classics like **The Grim Adventures of Billy & Mandy** and **Scooby-Doo Mystery Incorporated** returning, there are still some glaring absences. Shows like **Johnny Bravo**, **Courage the Cowardly Dog**, and **Ed, Edd n Eddy** are nowhere to be seen. Wondering what's up with that and if there's any word on them making a return?

**NostalgiaNerd**: Definitely missed seeing **Courage the Cowardly Dog** on the list. With the channel revamps and the re-introduction of other classics, you'd think they'd bring back the entire ensemble of fan favorites. Maybe they're spacing out re-introductions for a more staggered surprise effect?

**ToonCollector**: I was really hoping **Ed, Edd n Eddy** would make the cut this time around. Perhaps it has something to do with licensing issues or they're gauging audience interest for future comebacks? Either way, it's slightly disappointing, but the current lineup isn't too shabby either!

**SaturdayMorningFan**: It's a bit of a mixed bag, isn't it? On one hand, it's fantastic to have some of these shows back, but on the other, the absence of others leaves a bit of a gap. **Johnny Bravo**'s absence, in particular, caught me off guard. That show was a staple of my childhood Boomerang experience.

**RetroToonLover**: Could be a strategic move by Boomerang to see how well the current batch of returning shows performs before bringing back more. I'd suggest reaching out on social media or directly to the channel to express interest in seeing these other shows return. Viewer feedback might just be the nudge they need!

**ClassicToonFan**: That's a solid point, @RetroToonLover. Channels do listen to audience feedback, especially in this digital age where viewer engagement is more transparent than ever. For now, I'm just happy to see **World of Quest** and **Zig & Sharko** again, but fingers crossed that fan favorites missing from the lineup make their triumphant return soon.

**AnimationAficionado**: It's all about patience, I guess. The lineup revival is definitely exciting, and while not all our favorites made it back this time, who knows what the future holds? Boomerang's clearly on a path of nostalgia meets new horizons, so let's keep the feedback coming and hope for more good news in the following months.

**ToonTalker**: Exactly, let's keep the conversation going and our spirits high! It's a win having some of these shows back, and with enough support and interest, who knows? We might just get the full roster of classics in the near future. For now, let's enjoy the nostalgia trip and keep rooting for those yet to return.
Write the 1st of October schedule

### Welcome Back to Your Favorites: Boomerang's October 1st Special Schedule

Boomerang is rolling out the red carpet for the cherished classics making their grand return on October 1st! Gear up for a day filled with nostalgia, laughter, and adventures as we celebrate the comeback of some of your most beloved shows. Mark your calendars and prepare for a trip down memory lane with this special schedule:

#### Morning Kickoff

- **6:00 AM - Zig & Sharko**: Start your morning with the high-energy antics of Zig, Sharko, and Marina in their endless island escapades.
  
- **7:00 AM - Scooby-Doo Mystery Incorporated**: Join Scooby and the gang as they unravel the spooky mysteries of Crystal Cove, the most haunted place on earth.

- **8:00 AM - The Grim Adventures of Billy & Mandy**: Delve into the darkly comic and supernatural world of Billy, Mandy, and the Grim Reaper in Endsville.

#### Mid-Morning Adventures

- **9:00 AM - World of Quest**: Follow Prince Nestor and Quest in their epic quest to save the Kingdom of Odyssia.

- **10:00 AM - Smeshariki**: Dive into the whimsical world of Smeshariki, where simple stories pack profound life lessons.

#### Lunchtime Laughs

- **11:00 AM - Time Squad**: Buck Tuddrussel, Larry 3000, and Otto Osworth, time-travel to ensure history's greatest figures stay on track.

- **12:00 PM - Wacky Races**: Rev up your engines with the most wacky racers ever, competing for the title of "World's Wackiest Racer."

#### Afternoon Adventures

- **1:00 PM - Zig & Sharko (Encore Performance)**: Because you can never have too much of this hilarious trio's oceanic adventures!

- **2:00 PM - The Grim Adventures of Billy & Mandy (Encore Performance)**: Get a second dose of supernatural mishaps with Billy, Mandy, and Grim.

#### Prime Time Highlights

- **3:00 PM - Scooby-Doo Mystery Incorporated (Encore Performance)**: More mysteries, more snacks, and more Scooby in this encore presentation.

- **4:00 PM - World of Quest (Encore Performance)**: Rejoin the heroic duo on their quest in this action-packed encore.

- **5:00 PM - Smeshariki (Encore Performance)**: End your afternoon with the heartwarming tales of the round, fluffy creatures.

#### Evening Encore

- **6:00 PM - Time Squad (Encore Performance)**: Travel through time once more with the Time Squad in their mission to correct history's mishaps.

- **7:00 PM - Wacky Races (Encore Performance)**: Catch the second lap of the wackiest race on earth in this evening encore.

#### Late Night Rewind

- **8:00 PM - Prime Selection**: A handpicked selection of the best episodes from today’s showcased series, ensuring your night is filled with non-stop entertainment.

#### Special Showcase

- **10:00 PM - Fan Favorite Marathon**: As the clock strikes ten, settle in for a marathon of episodes from today’s lineup, chosen by you, the fans, via online polls.


Boomerang is thrilled to bring back these timeless shows to create new memories and rekindle the old ones. October 1st is just the beginning, so stay tuned for more adventures and laughter in the coming days!
